_comment: Canonical 1-100 suitability ratings for this role — the SOURCE OF TRUTH. HTML (dossier headlines + by-role ladders + peer tables) must match. Editorial judgement, not coriolis game data. Rebuild: scripts/compute-ship-ratings.py · push to HTML: scripts/reconcile-ratings-html.py · verify: scripts/audit-ratings-consistency.py

scorecard.verdict: The role ceiling: the largest premium cabin loadout in the game paired with the Saud Kruger comfort bonus for the highest gross per trip — short only on range and large-pad-only reach.

factors.why: Twelve optional internals carry ~184 economy berths plus a full first/business/luxury suite — the largest premium cabin loadout in the game for high-value VIP work.
A purpose-built Saud Kruger liner carries the passenger-comfort bonus: fares pay more, complain less and rate the trip higher than any converted freighter — the highest gross per run.
~40 LY engineered is the clear weak axis — a poor traveller to the most distant scenic spots, where the longer-legged Anaconda wins.
Six utility mounts stack Heavy-Duty boosters on a fast-charging bi-weave plus point defence to keep VIPs safe — strong, just short of the Cutter's warship shield.
Large-pad only (no outposts), slow and ponderous at 197/276 m/s, ~115M Cr fully fitted — the medium Lynx Highliner reaches outpost loops it can't.

scorecard.verdict: A dedicated Saud Kruger medium liner that leads on comfort and medium-pad reach with ~100 seats, held off 95 only by a hold smaller than the large Beluga's ~184.

factors.why: Built-up ~100 economy passengers across size-6/5/5/5/4 optional slots — strong for a dedicated liner and ahead of the small Dolphin (~42), but well under the large Beluga (~184) and even converted mediums like the Python (~146). Class fit is excellent: a purpose-built liner hull, not a converted cabin-stuffer.

Carries the Saud Kruger dedicated-liner comfort bonus — passengers pay premium and complain least, so profit per seat beats any converted hull (Cutter, Anaconda, Python). Comfort is its single strongest attribute, matched only by Beluga/Orca/Dolphin among liners.

Engineered FSD (size-5 SCO, Increased Range + Mass Manager) reaches ~38 LY built up from ~16 LY stock, on a fixed 32t (5C) fuel tank. Adequate for the short Robigo-Sirius loop but unremarkable next to long-legged converted hulls like the Krait Phantom.

6C bi-weave shield (Reinforced + Fast Charge) plus a 0A Heavy Duty shield booster covers passenger safety on threatened routes, but raw MJ falls far short of the Imperial Cutter's apex shields. Two 1F pulse lasers are token defence — adequate, not a strength.

Medium pad lands at outposts the large Orca and Beluga cannot reach — its defining edge for varied sightseeing destinations — with no rank or permit gate. ~68M Cr hull (~85M+ built up, ~3.5M rebuy) is moderate, and the pre-built free rebuy survives in-place engineering.

scorecard.verdict: Role-leading on raw berths (~340) and shields (~2,500 MJ) carry it to 89; the absent liner comfort bonus, capping per-seat pay, is the only real drag.

factors.why: ~340 economy berths is the most of any ship in the game, well past the Beluga's ~184, across ten optional slots (8·8·6·6·6·5·5·4·3·1); the two size-8 slots under-fill on size-6 cabins yet still top the field for raw capacity.

No liner comfort bonus — Gutamaya never built it as a tourist ship, so per-seat fares trail the Beluga, Lynx and Orca despite the larger headcount; first/luxury suites earn less here than on a dedicated liner.

~26 LY laden jump engineered (G5 Increased Range on a 7A FSD with Mass Manager offsetting the 1,100 t hull) is strong for a megaliner, beating slow short-legged rivals like the Beluga; fuel tank is a fixed 6C and cannot be engineered.

Strongest shields in the role — ~703 MJ base scaling to ~2,500+ MJ with an 8A Prismatic plus six Heavy-Duty boosters across eight utility mounts, backed by two 5D hull reinforcements; no other passenger hull survives interdiction like this.

Large-pad-bound and the priciest liner to outfit — ~209M Cr hull, ~451M fitted, behind an Imperial Duke rank gate and ~23M Cr rebuy; offset by 323 m/s boost to outrun trouble and a shared Imperial rank ladder that makes the gate dual-use.
